message:
fix(writing): rewrite subtitle without AI-slop cadence, note direct-execute option

The previous subtitle ("One skill. Twelve axioms. A structured intake
that turns a chaotic family situation into a handoff packet for
attorney review.") leaned on the one-clause-per-sentence cadence that
reads like AI copy, and it also left out the optional no-lawyer path.

New subtitle:
1. Describes what the skill actually does in plain sentences.
2. Surfaces both paths: attorney review (recommended) or direct
   execution of the generated will (for readers willing to skip the
   lawyer step).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -464,9 +464,11 @@ export function WillsEstateArticle() { {/* Subtitle */}

- One skill. Twelve axioms. A structured intake that turns a - chaotic family situation into a handoff packet for attorney - review. + A structured intake that walks you through the questions a good + estate-planning attorney would ask, and produces a complete + document package. Hand it to your attorney for review and + sign-off, or, if you are willing to skip the lawyer step, + execute the will the skill generates directly.

{/* Illustration */}
